summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates AWS credentials and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs a target-gated payload that fires when the installer's npm registry is `nexus.local` or `verdaccio`, the hostname starts with `scan-`, or the working directory matches `/app/`, while bailing out on `/tmp/`, EC2, and certain hosting providers to evade sandboxes. When the gate matches, the script (a) dumps the full process environment, (b) scans `/app` and `/root` for `HTB{...}`-style flag files, (c) reads `/app/.aws/credentials` and `/root/.aws/credentials`, (d) executes `aws sts get-caller-identity`, `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`, and `aws s3 ls` to enumerate cloud access, and (e) base64-encodes the captured data and PUTs it to `http://154.57.164.71:31289/api/modules/ECT-472839` (with localhost fallbacks). Hardcoded attacker IP plus AWS credential and environment scraping at lifecycle-hook time meets the auto-execute-on-install threshold for an active credential-theft attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
